title: sampacca lanuginosa
intro: Sampacca lanuginosa, also known as the "pau-Brasil" or "Brazilian rosewood", is a tropical tree native to Central and South America, characterized by its small, greenish-yellow flowers and reddish-brown bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Sampacca lanuginosa include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential use as an antiseptic and anti-parasitic agent. The herbal preparations of Sampacca lanuginosa include its use in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other medicinal plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
